name: HEART Score
full_name: History, ECG, Age, Risk factors, Troponin Score
description: Estimates risk of major adverse cardiac events in patients with acute chest pain.
purpose: Identify emergency department patients with acute chest pain for early discharge or further cardiac testing.
when_to_use: Adult emergency department patients with symptoms related to acute coronary syndrome
Patients with acute chest pain without ST-elevation on ECG
Deciding whether to discharge patients early or pursue further cardiac testing
when_not_to_use: Patients with ST-elevation myocardial infarction on ECG
Patients with clear alternative diagnosis for chest pain

items.label: history
items.description: History: Slightly suspicious, Moderately suspicious, Highly suspicious

items.label: ecg
items.description: ECG: Normal, Non-specific repolarization disturbance, Significant ST depression

items.label: risk_factors
items.description: Risk factors (≥3 risk factors or history of atherosclerotic disease): No known risk factors, 1-2 risk factors, ≥3 risk factors or history of atherosclerotic disease

items.label: troponin
items.description: Troponin: Normal limit, 1-3× normal limit, >3× normal limit

interpretation.description: Low risk. Consider for early discharge with serial troponin at 0 and 3 hours. If both troponins negative, patient can be safely discharged without further cardiac testing.
Moderate risk. Admit for further observation and cardiac testing (stress testing or angiography).
High risk. Strongly consider early invasive measures including angiography.

references.citation: Mahler SA, Riley RF, Hiestand BC, Russell GB, Hoekstra JW, Lefebvre CW, Nicks BA, Cline DM, Askew KL, Elliott SB, Herrington DM, Burke GL, Miller CD. The HEART Pathway randomized trial: identifying emergency department patients with acute chest pain for early discharge. Circ Cardiovasc Qual Outcomes. 2015 Mar;8(2):195-203.

constraints.description: Patient can only be in one age category

notes: The HEART Pathway combines the HEART score with troponin measurements at 0 and 3 hours. Patients with HEART score 0-3 and negative troponins at both time points are considered low risk for early discharge. The score was validated to predict major adverse cardiac events (death, myocardial infarction, or coronary revascularization) within 30 days.
